transcript.clean_text: the day after the tourmalade calm ensuing it seemed at the beginning of the next stage 14. and the hometown start for Cyril Bart but all eyes were on Remco of Nepal a man who'd virtually collapsed it seemed on the tourmalade when yumbo visma had completely taken control of the race what would lay ahead destination Lara malagua well a whole sequence of climbs after a very flat start to the day in comparison to what has been before this would draw out a breakaway of what composition nobody knew the flag fell temperatures were high around 32 Celsius on occasion and the racing was white hot from the very beginning and one Rider seemed to be involved in just about every move Remco evenapur well after his apparent disintegration on the tourmalade this was something remarkable just 24 hours later he was busy racing and not only that dominating every single incarnation of the Breakaway the Belgian Champion was involved and he had some good company as well in fact eventually the Breakaway was to swell to a group of around 24 Riders pursued by other groups that had equally as effective riders for a day such as this meanwhile in the red jersey group curse being protected as you'd expect and indeed the top 10 or less riding together even Nepal was out for mountains points and potentially more than that we just didn't know almost his eternal partner on a really tough challenge going from this sort of distance was something well only the greats can take on bardet was trying to help in the end it was just water really he could offer up to even a poll who was in such determined mood a man who doesn't really know how to quantify himself on occasion maybe this is one day where we'll be asking the same questions ourselves there was a few others out there that wanted to be part of the game Michael storero was hoping to take control of the king of the mountains Jersey he was part of very much a Chase Group found himself isolated on occasion and was picking up minor points meanwhile I use though thought about stretching his legs because under also very much involved hoping to improve his position overall maybe a top five but what about the day itself how would that pan out well the climbs went the climbs leading to downhills on a rough day the ayuza wanted to at least gain something from but couldn't find any space marked out every single time by the teammates of sepkus and having a Paul and bow day continued to drive on the gap on the pursuers from the Breakaway of a minute and 46 and swelling to over six minutes on yet another descent that would take us to our final climb well the toughest part of the day now behind bar day and even Apple but how would it pan out in the end an extra kick inside the four kilometers to go and even a poll was going to produce one of the most remarkable comebacks that cycling has ever witnessed the emotions were bound to rise towards the four we were wondering how he might celebrate in the end it was Remco the Redeemer as he crossed the line tears would follow but what a remarkable performance one that will live long in the memory of everyone who witnessed it a remarkable performance by a truly remarkable Rider nearest man to in over a minute down there's been some bravery out there there was still a GC battle they thought potentially going to rage behind this pair in the end it was all benign than that they had stretched their legs in opposition against each other but no gaps were found and so the top ten staying more or less exactly the same Ava Nepal taking the most remarkable return to form that anyone has ever witnessed and in so doing getting himself a king of the mountains Jersey along the way there will be more to come from this man potentially within this race as well expect the unexpected the mountains classification led by even a Paul and he now may well start targeting that Storer nearest to him starting off as the day's favorite with Ava Nepal I think the favorites in many people's hearts by the end of this of course gathered around him when asked whether sepsis was indeed the team leader he on his finger go said of course and he leads this race by a minute and 37. roglitch being a girl making up a team jumbo visma Podium that might go all the way to Madrid climbing of course still to come and indeed a testy day before we even get to our rest day Stage 15 from Pamplona destination looking Berry more climbs to come not as tough as today but certainly one that will draw some action every single day appears to do precisely that olympi start a breakaway in the offing will ever Nepal be up the road possibly not but you never know no more thank you
